title: Coffins
description: Adds coffins to the game, inspired by other gravestone/tombstone mods that store your items upon death, which can also be used as storage unit. Includes Trinkets Support!
body:
![Title & Version](https://cdn.modrinth.com/data/cached_images/3f1606b843306aa56fb11b95d5d2eefbae0fe240.png)


# **_CURRENTLY UNDERGOING A REWRITE_**

## COFFINS
Coffins is a fabric mod, that adds coffins to the game! They _store your items upon death_, so you don't have to be worried about losing them! They _can also be crafted_ and be used as a storage unit.

## KNOWN (IN)COMPATIBLE MODS
### COMPATIBLE
- Requiem (only works when dying as a player, not as a mob)
- Trinkets (has built-in support starting at v1.2.0)
### INCOMPATIBLE
- Sinytra

## FULL FEATURE LIST
### New Storage Block: Coffin
- spawns upon death to store dropped items
- can be crafted
- has a total of 54 slots
- when fragile, disappears once emptied
- can be locked using the Gravedigger's Key
- has a dark mode option
### New Items:
#### Tome of Fragility
- when used on a Coffin, turns it fragile
- can be acquired over buried treasure, fishing or ancient cities
#### Gravedigger's Key
- can be used to lock a Coffin, making it inaccessable to other players
### Gamerules
- showDeathCoordinates: Controls whether or not to show Death Coordinates to the player upon dying
- keepRecoveryCompass: Controls whether or not to keep the Recovery Compass upon dying
- allowCoffinRobbing: Controls if other players are allowed to look into other player's coffins (Death only)
- deathRequiresCoffin: Controls if a coffin should spawn only if the player has one on them (On Death)
### Other Tweaks
- when holding a recovery compass, coordinates and distance to your last death location
- will be displayed
- echo shards may now be crafted using amethyst shards and sculk blocks

## SUGGESTIONS / ISSUES
Have something to suggest or found a bug? Either [join our Discord](https://discord.gg/Dmp2dbyNrs) or [make an issue in our GitHub](https://github.com/Now-this-is-crazy/Coffins/issues).
